solution.p: To add two fractions with different denominators, we first have to find a common denominator.
In this case, the least common multiple of 10 and 6 is 30.
Look at the first fraction \displaystyle{\frac{3}{10}}. To change the denominator to 30, we multiply 3 at both the top and bottom, and we have:
Similarly, we change the second fractions denominator to 30:
Now we can add those two fractions. The full solution is:
